title: Charred Crispy Egyptian Epoisses Paella
description: A unique and flavorful paella dish from Egypt, featuring the strong and pungent Epoisses cheese, cured with shio-koji for added depth.

ingredients.name: Epoisses cheese
ingredients.notes: Strong and pungent cheese, cured with shio-koji for added depth.

ingredients.name: Shio-koji
ingredients.notes: Japanese rice starter used for curing the Epoisses cheese.

ingredients.name: Paleo-friendly saffron
ingredients.notes: Used for its distinctive flavor and color.

ingredients.name: Paleo-friendly paprika
ingredients.notes: Adds a smoky depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Used for greasing the paella pan.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Diced and saut 4d before adding the rice.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced and saut 4d before adding the rice.

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.notes: Paella rice is preferred for its unique texture.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped and used as a garnish.

ingredients.name: Cherry tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Halved and added to the paella for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Shallots
ingredients.notes: Thinly sliced and added to the paella for added flavor.

instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic and saut 4 until translucent.
Add the lamb broth and bring to a boil.
Add the rice and stir to coat with the broth.
Add the Epoisses cheese, shio-koji, paleo-friendly saffron, and paleo-friendly paprika, and stir to combine.
Transfer the pan to the oven and cook for 25 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the top is crispy.
Remove from the oven and stir in the chopped fresh parsley and halved cherry tomatoes.
Serve hot, garnished with thinly sliced shallots.
Let the paella rest for 5 minutes before serving, allowing the flavors to meld together.
